name: Pagination
summary: The blocks-layer home for the Collection Operations `page` stage. Renders paged controls inside a `<nav aria-label="pagination">` landmark with `aria-current="page"`, syncs the active page to a shareable self-canonical `?page=n` URL via the History API (Navigation API as enhancement; cross-document View Transitions for zero-JS server-rendered transitions), and wires `advance:auto` to an IntersectionObserver scroll sentinel. Numbered / prev-next / load-more presentations over the orthogonal `pageMode` × `advance` axes; infinite scroll = append + auto, never a peer mode. Ships a consumable `PaginationBehavior` (and a `<page-nav>` element) that mounts the verified renderer, delegates page / load-more clicks, clamps + advances the page state, and emits `pagination-change`; first consumer is the loan-origination pipeline (#317).

paginationNavLandmark.pattern: Pagination controls wrapped in a `<nav aria-label="pagination">` landmark; the active page link marked `aria-current="page"`
paginationNavLandmark.adoption: PaginationBehavior renders the nav landmark and projects the active page as aria-current='page'; each landmark gets a unique label when more than one exists

ariaCurrent.pattern: aria-current='page' marks the active page within the pagination nav
ariaCurrent.adoption: Set on the active page link; the companion sortDirection->aria-sort projection on table headers is deferred to the grid block

historyApi.pattern: pushState/popstate/scrollRestoration sync ?page=n to a shareable, back-button-restorable URL
historyApi.adoption: The native-first baseline for urlSync='query-param'; the active page is a self-canonical ?page=n and restores on back/forward

navigationApi.pattern: A single navigate event intercepts same-origin same-document navigations
navigationApi.adoption: Progressive enhancement over the History API baseline — used when available, never required

crossDocumentViewTransitions.pattern: @view-transition { navigation: auto; } animates full-page navigation between server-rendered ?page=n pages with zero JS
crossDocumentViewTransitions.adoption: The MPA/server-rendered branch opts in via the CSS rule on both pages; no script required

intersectionObserver.pattern: A scroll sentinel observed by IntersectionObserver requests the next slice at the scroll boundary
intersectionObserver.adoption: Wires advance:auto (append + auto = infinite scroll); rendering delegates to Windowed Collection, lifecycle to Loader

selfCanonicalUrls.pattern: Each page is its own unique self-referencing canonical ?page=n; rel=next/prev is deprecated (2019) and never emitted as primary machinery
selfCanonicalUrls.adoption: Server-rendered <a href> page links are the indexable default; load-more/auto content provides a crawlable paginated path alongside the interactive UI

dedicatedBlockOverGrid.description: A dedicated pagination block realizing the `page` dimension family, rather than folding it into a broad grid/data-table block
dedicatedBlockOverGrid.rationale: Every concrete renderable in the gap is pagination (nav landmark, aria-current, page-size selector, range label, scroll sentinel). The filter/sort/group rendering home (and the sortDirection->aria-sort projection, a table-header concern) is a separate, larger block tracked independently. Keeps this contract bounded and demonstrable against the verified WAI-ARIA APG pattern.

pageModeXAdvance.description: Two orthogonal axes — pageMode (paged | append) x advance (manual | auto) — not one coupled `mode` list
pageModeXAdvance.rationale: Infinite scroll is a derived opt-in (append + auto), not a peer mode. paged + auto is undefined (you do not auto-replace a slice on scroll). Matches the Collection Operations intent vocabulary verbatim.

nativeFirstUrlState.description: History API is the baseline for urlSync; Navigation API and cross-document View Transitions are enhancements
nativeFirstUrlState.rationale: Native-first: the History API has been Baseline since 2020 and covers ?page=n + back/forward + scroll restoration with no library. The Navigation API (Baseline 2026) and zero-JS View Transitions layer on where supported.

seoLoadBearing.description: urlSync is load-bearing for append modes, not optional polish
seoLoadBearing.rationale: Googlebot does not fire user-action JS, so load-more/auto content is not reliably crawlable; append modes are only advisable with query-param URL sync on, else the footer and back button break. Server-rendered self-canonical ?page=n links are the SEO-safe default; rel=next/prev is dead.

rangeLabelRequiresTotal.description: rangeLabel ('showing 21-40 of 500') and jump-to-page compose only over an offset/page resource protocol
rangeLabelRequiresTotal.rationale: A cursor protocol has no total, so it structurally forces prev-next or load-more and forces rangeLabel off. This cross-layer constraint is inherited from the Custom Pagination resource, not re-modeled here.
rangeLabelRequiresTotal.openQuestion: Recommended page-size options and defaults, and how the range label degrades with no total, are unverified — owned by backlog #060.

contractsOnly.description: This block defines the contract (nav structure, observable events, URL-state and advance strategies); concrete strategy implementations live in Frontier UI
contractsOnly.rationale: Web Everything is the standard. The block names the registry/strategy seams; competing implementations (a custom URL-state provider, an alternate advance trigger) register against them without changing the contract.

focusAfterPageChange.description: Focus and screen-reader announcement after a page change are intentionally unresolved here
focusAfterPageChange.rationale: The APG landmark + aria-current contract is verified; post-change focus/announcement is an open question tracked in backlog #059 and must not be guessed.

react.notes: TanStack models the page state; React Aria supplies the a11y wiring. We compose both into one declarative block over the intent.

vue.notes: Component-level numbered pagination with aria-current; no built-in cursor/offset protocol coupling.

github.notes: Server-rendered <a href> numbered links inside a nav landmark — closest to our SEO-safe default.

adobe.notes: Prior art for the result-range label and page-size selector affordances (defaults pending #060).

traits.name: withRangeLabel
traits.description: Surfaces the slice as a 'showing 21-40 of 500' result-range label. Requires a known total -> offset/page protocol only.

traits.name: withPageSizeSelect
traits.description: Adds a page-size selector (e.g. 10 / 25 / 50 / 100). Recommended options and persistence pending #060.

traits.name: withAutoAdvance
traits.description: Turns append into infinite scroll by fetching the next slice at an IntersectionObserver scroll sentinel (advance:auto).

traits.name: withUrlSync
traits.description: Reflects the active page in a shareable, back-button-restorable ?page=n URL via the History API.
